title: Cartograph - discover a land, draw a map, have an adventure (solo RPG)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Ocp1_PPM4sI

Daniel (@dungeondive) takes Brandon Lee's *Cartograph* to the table — a light solo RPG of map-making and discovery designed to be played *before* another game, so the map you produce becomes the setting for whatever system you adopt next. You need hex paper, three colours of d6 (landmarks, biomes, temporary), a journal, and a standard deck of cards including jokers.

Each turn you roll your dice pool onto the map near your current position. Landmark dice place settlements, towns, cities, or discoveries; biome dice fill the surrounding hexes. Drawing a card resolves the journey to your destination (suits map to four outcomes), and arriving in a settlement opens converse / rest / heal / restock / sell / shop actions, each costing one of your two free actions per stop (extras cost coin). Wounds tick up to four, dice can run out of the pool, and drawing a third joker of either colour ends the run — three ways for the cartographer to expire and a new one to inherit the legacy.

Daniel's session features a Servants of the Dead Eye cult, the ruined town of Greathold, and Gloomguard the weapon-trading city. He flags the biome table as the weakest link.

----

Does a map produced by *Cartograph* hold its shape when you migrate it into a heavier system, or do you end up redrawing it anyway?
